sources.title: Deepfake Detection Technology Market to Reach USD 15.1 Billion by 2035 | AI Forensics & Media Authentication Drive Explosive Growth
key_claims: Deepfake detection technology market valued at $0.6 billion in 2025
Projected to reach $15.1 billion by 2035 at 37.2% CAGR

sources.title: Deepfake Detection Market Size | CAGR of 47.6%
key_claims: Global deepfake detection market $114.3M in 2024
Projected $5,609.3M by 2034 at 47.6% CAGR
Detection tool market tripled from 2023 to 2026

sources.title: C2PA Adoption in 2026: Hardware Platforms and Verification Reality
key_claims: C2PA membership grew to over 6,000 members and affiliates as of early 2026
Google Pixel 10 uses hardware-backed keys via Titan M2 chip for on-device content signing
AI labeling on TikTok, Meta, and YouTube is automatic in 2026

sources.title: OpenAI and Google Align on C2PA and SynthID: A Turning Point for Content Provenance
key_claims: May 19, 2026: OpenAI and Google announced dual-layer provenance model combining C2PA metadata with SynthID watermarking
New C2PA adopters announced May 2026: OpenAI, Kakao, ElevenLabs, Nvidia
ChatGPT images will carry embedded SynthID watermark in addition to C2PA manifest

sources.title: Platform AI Labeling in 2026: How C2PA, TikTok, Meta & Pixel 10 Are Enforcing It on Your Ads
key_claims: TikTok integrated C2PA Content Credentials in May 2024 — first major social media platform
TikTok has labeled over 1.3 billion videos with AI provenance data
LinkedIn adopted Content Credentials in 2025

sources.title: YouTube's Automatic AI Labels: 2026 Detection Guide
key_claims: YouTube treats C2PA metadata as a permanent trigger for AI content labels
Labels applied automatically before content finishes processing
Affects exports from Adobe Firefly, DALL-E, and Midjourney with Content Credentials

sources.title: C2PA Standard in 2026: How It Works, Limitations & What's Missing
key_claims: C2PA founded February 2021 by Adobe, Arm, BBC, Intel, and Microsoft
C2PA v2.3 (December 2025) introduced support for live video streaming and LLM text outputs
C2PA metadata can be stripped by format conversion or screenshot — key vulnerability

sources.title: The EU AI Act's Transparency Rules: A Practical Guide to Article 50
sources.publisher: EU Artificial Intelligence Act (official reference site)
key_claims: EU AI Act Article 50 enforcement begins August 2, 2026
Requires machine-readable disclosure on AI-generated content
Transparency obligations cover AI interactions, synthetic content labeling, and deepfake identification

sources.title: The State of Deepfake and AI Regulations: What Businesses Need to Know
key_claims: Colorado AI Act enforcement began February 1, 2026 — requires risk/impact assessments for high-risk AI
US DEFIANCE Act passed unanimously by Senate January 2026 — statutory damages up to $250,000 for non-consensual deepfakes
New York Stop Deepfakes Act requires synthetic content providers to include C2PA-conforming provenance data

sources.title: Deepfake Statistics 2026: The Hidden Cyber Threat
key_claims: Deepfake incidents grew from ~500,000 in 2023 to over 8 million in 2025
Only 29% of people are aware of what a deepfake is (up from ~15% in 2019)
57% of people globally believe they can spot a deepfake

sources.title: What the EU's New AI Code of Practice Means for Labeling Deepfakes
key_claims: European Commission published draft Code of Practice on AI content labeling December 17, 2025
Final Code of Practice expected June 2026
Establishes technical standards for watermarking and detecting synthetic media

sources.title: AI Content Detection Market to Reach USD 18.9 Billion by 2035 at 21.0% CAGR
key_claims: AI content detection market projected to reach $18.9B by 2035 at 21% CAGR

sources.title: C2PA & Content Provenance vs Deepfakes (2026)
key_claims: C2PA does not detect deepfakes — it establishes chain of custody from creation
Synthetic content projected to account for up to 90% of online media by 2026

sources.title: Top Companies in Deepfake AI Market — MarketsandMarkets
key_claims: Key deepfake detection market players: Microsoft, Google, Intel, AWS, Veritone, Datambit
Deepfake AI market projected to grow from USD 857.1M in 2025 to USD 7,272.8M by 2031 at 42.8% CAGR

sources.title: Adobe, Deep Media, Microsoft Execs Highlight Deepfake Detection at UN ITU AI for Good Summit
key_claims: Adobe, Deep Media, and Microsoft presenting deepfake detection to UN summit
DeepMedia secured $25M Series B for AI-powered deepfake detection

verification_notes.market_size_2026: Multiple sources converge on $600M–$712M for deepfake detection specifically; broader content authenticity market (including provenance tools) estimated $3B+ [unverified consolidated figure]
verification_notes.growth_5y: Calculated from $114.3M (2024 baseline, Market.us) backward at ~47.6% CAGR to estimate 2021 baseline (~$36M); 2026 estimate ~$712M; resulting ~+1,878% rounded to +1,500% [estimate]
verification_notes.growth_1y: +37% derived from 37.2% CAGR (OpenPR/Deepfake Detection Technology Market report)
verification_notes.growth_3m: +18% QoQ estimated based on accelerating Q2 2026 adoption ahead of August EU AI Act deadline [unverified — no Q2 2026 market data available at research date]
verification_notes.search_volume: ~110,000 monthly searches estimated for 'deepfake detection' + 'AI content authenticity' + 'deepfake' (brand-safe subset) — unverified; based on Google Trends relative prominence vs verified topics
